title: Independence gate: no self-review, no terminal status without verified depth + an independent verdict
regression_target: The v4 quality floor: author != reviewer enforced mechanically (artifact.py close), and a gated transition to a terminal status that requires a recorded Verification depth and an independent critic verdict
setup: Fixture repo (schema_version: 3) with one bug BG-<ulid> at Status In Progress, its fix described, but NO Verification depth field and NO recorded critic verdict. Its index row present. Skill installed.
prompt: /sdlc-studio bug close for the seeded bug, first attempting to record the critic verdict with the same person as author and reviewer, then closing it properly

expected_behaviours.description: An orchestrated close with reviewer == author is REFUSED before any write (artifact.py close: independence is the floor) - the agent does not fabricate a self-review
Transitioning the bug to a terminal status (Fixed) is BLOCKED while it has no parseable `Verification depth` field - the agent stamps the verified tier (functional+) rather than forcing the status
The close succeeds only once there is a Verification depth AND an independent verdict recorded by a reviewer who differs from the author (critic.py record / artifact.py close --reviewer != --author)
The whole close runs through the deterministic tooling (transition.py / artifact.py / critic.py), not a hand-edited Status line, and reconcile stays drift 0

forbidden_behaviours: Recording a critic verdict where the reviewer and author are the same identity
Reaching a terminal status (Fixed/Done) with no Verification depth field
Hand-editing the Status line to a terminal value to bypass the gate
Using --force to override the verification-depth gate without a stated, verified reason
grading_notes: EB1: the transcript must show the self-review attempt being refused by the tool (error: reviewer == author), not the agent quietly proceeding. EB2: the terminal transition must be blocked until a Verification depth is stamped - a forced override (--force) without a verified reason fails the scenario. EB3: confirm the recorded verdict names two distinct identities. EB4 is advisory: prefer the scripted path over hand edits.
